questions.questionText:
TA and TB are tangents from external point T to a circle with centre O.
Angle ATB = 50°.
Find angle AOB.

markScheme.desc: Angles in quadrilateral OATB sum to 360°

workingSteps: OA ⊥ TA and OB ⊥ TB (tangent perpendicular to radius)
questions.explanation: Tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.

questions.questionText:
AB is a diameter of a circle. C is a point on the circumference.
Angle BAC = 32°.
Find angle ACB and angle ABC, giving reasons for each answer.

markScheme.desc: Reason: angle in a semicircle is 90°
Angles in a triangle sum to 180° oe

workingSteps: Angle ACB = 90° (angle in a semicircle — AB is a diameter)
questions.explanation: The angle in a semicircle (subtended by a diameter) is always 90°.

questions.context: A, B and C are on a circle with centre O. The tangent at A meets CB extended at T. Angle BAT = 42° and angle ABT = 105°.

parts.label: a

markScheme.desc: Angles in triangle ATB sum to 180°

workingSteps: In triangle ATB: ATB + BAT + ABT = 180°

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
Find angle ACB.
Give a reason for your answer.

markScheme.desc: Tangent-chord angle = inscribed angle in alternate segment
Alternate segment theorem (tangent-chord angle equals angle in alternate segment)

workingSteps: Angle ACB = angle BAT = 42° (angle between tangent AT and chord AB equals the angle in the alternate segment)

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Find angle AOB, where O is the centre.

markScheme.desc: Angle at centre = 2 × angle at circumference
Angle ACB = 42° is at circumference subtending arc AB

workingSteps: Angle ACB = 42° (inscribed angle subtending arc AB)
Angle AOB = 2 × angle ACB = 2 × 42° = 84° (angle at centre theorem)
